message:
feat(trace/distributed): add vector clocks, convergent lattice, and sheaf consistency checker

Three new modules implementing the distributed tracing infrastructure
for Phase 4 (Distributed Trace - Causal Ordering + Convergent State):

- vclock.rs (564 lines): Vector clock implementation for causal ordering.
  Maps NodeId -> u64 counters with partial order comparison, merge
  (componentwise max), increment, and CausalTracker for per-event
  clock management. Supports happens-before, concurrent, and equal
  ordering relations.

- lattice.rs (634 lines): Join-semilattice for distributed obligation
  and lease state convergence. ObligationLattice merges replicated
  state using CRDT-style rules: Unknown < Reserved < Committed/Aborted,
  with Committed ⊔ Aborted = Conflict (protocol violation). Also
  includes LeaseLatticeState for distributed lease convergence.

- sheaf.rs (576 lines): Sheaf-theoretic consistency checker that detects
  global inconsistencies missed by pairwise checks. Uses the concept
  of local sections (per-node observations), phantom commits (states
  committed in merge but never observed by any single node), and
  constraint violations (saga atomicity requirements that hold pairwise
  but fail globally). Reports H¹ ≠ 0 style obstructions.

- distributed/mod.rs: Updated module doc and re-exports for all new
  types (VectorClock, CausalTracker, LatticeState, ObligationLattice,
  SagaConsistencyChecker, NodeSnapshot, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
